quick_facts.label: Service and cadre
quick_facts.value: The IAS is an All India Service. Its cadre framework includes State and Joint Cadres, with cadre posts encompassing senior administrative roles such as District Officers/Collectors, Commissioners, and Secretaries or Deputy Secretaries in administrative departments.

quick_facts.label: Primary direct-entry route
quick_facts.value: Direct recruitment is through a competitive examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) under the IAS recruitment framework.

quick_facts.label: Other statutory entry routes
quick_facts.value: IAS recruitment rules also provide for promotion of substantive members of a State Civil Service and, in special cases, selection from eligible officers holding substantive gazetted State posts who are not State Civil Service members.

quick_facts.label: Induction training
quick_facts.value: For direct recruits, LBSNAA’s induction pathway includes the Foundation Course, approximately 20 weeks of IAS Professional Course Phase I, district training, an Assistant Secretary programme, and IAS Professional Course Phase II.

quick_facts.label: 2026 examination context
quick_facts.value: For the 2026 Civil Services (Preliminary) Examination, UPSC listed notification on 4 February 2026, the examination on 24 May 2026, and the application deadline as 27 February 2026 at 6:00 pm; these dates are cycle-specific and already past as of 19 July 2026.

sections.heading: What an IAS officer does
paragraphs: IAS officers undertake broad public-administration assignments within their allotted State or Joint Cadre and may hold senior posts in district administration and state administrative departments. The cadre rules identify posts such as District Officers/Collectors, Commissioners, Members of the Board of Revenue, and Secretaries or Deputy Secretaries as roles requiring the wide administrative experience associated with the IAS.

Early-career preparation is designed around managing public systems and administrative responsibilities. LBSNAA identifies legal instruments, administrative rules and programme guidelines, management tools, economic analysis, communication, and an understanding of the allotted state’s social, cultural, and administrative context as relevant capabilities.
bullets: Administering district-level and departmental public functions in accordance with applicable laws, rules, and programme guidelines.
Supporting implementation and management of public systems and government programmes.
Working across socio-economic, political, legal, and organisational contexts, including with other public institutions and services.

sections.heading: How to enter the IAS
paragraphs: The principal route for a new graduate or other eligible applicant is the UPSC Civil Services Examination (CSE). The IAS Appointment by Competitive Examination Regulations define the examination as a combined competitive examination consisting of a preliminary examination and a main examination, conducted by UPSC in the manner notified by the Central Government.

The statutory framework also recognises career-entry routes from within state government: promotion of substantive State Civil Service members, and selection in special cases from qualifying holders of substantive gazetted State posts who are not members of a State Civil Service. These are service-based routes, not alternatives an external applicant can ordinarily apply for directly.

Applicants should rely on the annual UPSC examination notice for the applicable year’s eligibility, reservation, attempt, medical, application, examination, and service-allocation conditions. For context only, the 2026 preliminary examination deadline was 27 February 2026 and the examination was held on 24 May 2026.
bullets: Direct recruitment: qualify through the UPSC Civil Services Examination and receive allocation to IAS under the applicable process.
Promotion route: eligible substantive State Civil Service officers may be considered under the promotion regulations.
Selection route: special-case selection may be available for eligible substantive gazetted State officers outside the State Civil Service, subject to the governing regulations.

sections.heading: Skills developed and used
paragraphs: LBSNAA’s IAS Phase I curriculum signals the core professional capabilities expected during the first decade of service: application of law and legal instruments; administrative procedures and programme guidelines; modern management tools; economic analysis; regional-language proficiency; written and oral communication; public-service values; and physical fitness.

The training also emphasises a pan-India perspective, understanding public systems and their management, and appreciation of the cultural and socio-economic setting of the allotted cadre. These are particularly important for officers who must work with communities, state departments, local bodies, and other institutions.
bullets: Public administration, programme implementation, and systems management.
Legal and procedural reasoning.
Economic and policy analysis.
Clear written, oral, interpersonal, and organisational communication.
Regional-language learning and contextual understanding of the allotted state.
Ethical judgement, values, and professional resilience.

sections.heading: Where and how IAS officers work
paragraphs: IAS officers are allotted to State or Joint Cadres and serve in public-administration posts that can range from district assignments to senior state-secretariat and departmental roles. The cadre rules specifically contemplate senior administrative posts at and above the district-officer level.

The direct-recruit training sequence combines academy-based instruction with field exposure. LBSNAA states that after Phase I, officer trainees proceed to one year of district training in their allotted cadres. Its Phase I winter study tour also includes attachments with organisations such as the armed forces, public sector, private sector, municipal bodies, voluntary agencies, tribal areas, e-governance institutions, and NGOs.
bullets: State or Joint Cadre administration, including district and departmental roles.
Field-facing work during district training and later public-administration assignments.
Coordination with multiple levels of government and a range of public, community, and institutional stakeholders.

sections.heading: Training and career progression
paragraphs: The IAS pay rules set out a grade structure from Junior Scale (Level 10) through Senior Time Scale (Level 11), Junior Administrative Grade (Level 12), Selection Grade (Level 13), Super Time Scale (Level 14), Higher Administrative Grade (Level 15), Apex Scale (Level 17), and the Cabinet Secretary grade (Level 18). Appointment to higher grades is governed by the applicable rules, conditions, and availability of posts; it is not an automatic guarantee of a particular posting.

As a dated regulatory reference, the 2016 IAS Pay Rules state eligibility milestones of four years for Senior Time Scale, nine years for Junior Administrative Grade, thirteen years for Selection Grade, and sixteen years for Super Time Scale, calculated from the officer’s year of allotment and subject to the rules’ conditions.
Professional development continues after induction. LBSNAA lists IAS Phase III, IV, and V mid-career training programmes alongside the direct-recruit induction stages.
bullets: Induction: Foundation Course, Phase I, district training, Assistant Secretary programme, and Phase II.
In-service development: common and IAS-specific mid-career training programmes.
Grade progression: governed by service rules, eligibility periods, assessment/selection requirements, and cadre-post availability.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Compensation is governed by the Indian Administrative Service (Pay) Rules, 2016 and subsequent applicable government orders. Under those rules, a direct recruit’s initial basic pay is fixed at the first cell of Pay Matrix Level 10 (Junior Scale); the rules then specify higher pay levels for subsequent IAS grades.

This is a regulatory pay-structure reference rather than a current take-home-pay estimate. Actual monthly remuneration can vary with the applicable pay-matrix cell, posting, admissible allowances, deductions, cadre-related conditions, and future government revisions. Candidates should check current official pay and allowance orders rather than rely on generic salary figures.
bullets: Entry grade for a direct recruit under the 2016 rules: Junior Scale, Pay Matrix Level 10.
Higher IAS grades in the rules span Levels 11–15, Level 17, and Level 18 for the Cabinet Secretary grade.
Basic pay and take-home pay are not the same; allowances and deductions are context-dependent.
